Document:
# Surgery
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Surgery"]
Surgery allows you to heal wounds, remove or replace body parts and organs, and perform advanced medical procedures. It is the most powerful tool in a Wasteland medic's kit — and the most dangerous if done incorrectly.
## Before You Begin
Your patient must be lying down and ideally sedated before you start. Operating on a conscious patient causes severe pain, worsens their mood, and makes wounds harder to manage.
Use [bold]Nitrous Oxide[/bold] with a breathing mask to sedate your patient safely before beginning any procedure.
You must also wear protective gear to prevent contaminating the patient's wounds:
[bold]Sterile Mask[/bold] — any surgical mask prevents airborne contamination
[bold]Nitrile Gloves[/bold] — any surgical gloves prevent contact contamination
## Surgical Tools
A full surgical kit contains everything needed to open, operate on, and close a patient safely. Most procedures require several tools used in sequence.
[bold]Scalpel[/bold] — makes the initial incision to open the surgical site
[bold]Retractor[/bold] — holds the incision open so you can work inside
[bold]Hemostat[/bold] — clamps bleeding vessels and assists with organ removal
[bold]Drill[/bold] — bores through bone when access to deeper structures is needed
[bold]Saw[/bold] — cuts through bone for amputation and structural work
[bold]Cautery[/bold] — seals wounds and closes incisions with heat
[bold]Bone Gel[/bold] — stabilizes and seals bone after drilling or fracture repair
Once you have tools, interact with your patient to begin. You will be prompted to choose which body part to operate on.
## Ghetto Surgery
When proper surgical tools are unavailable, improvised alternatives can substitute in a pinch. Examine the item to see how effective it is as a surgical substitute.
[bold]Welder[/bold] — cauterization substitute
[bold]Wirecutter[/bold] — hemostat substitute
[bold]Kitchen Knife[/bold] — scalpel substitute
[bold]Crowbar[/bold] — retractor substitute
Improvised tools are less effective than proper surgical equipment and increase the risk of complications. Use them only when nothing better is available.
